question: Which process instance states qualify for Operate's process instance migration?

options.text: Running instances only — that is, instances in an active or incident state.
Completed instances only, so their history can be re-played on a new version.
Cancelled instances only, so they can be revived on the target version.
Any state — active, completed, and cancelled — all migrate together.

a.text: The docs state explicitly that it is only possible to migrate running process instances, meaning instances in an active or incident state.

b.text: Completed instances are immutable history and are not part of any migration plan.

c.text: Cancelled instances are not running and are excluded from migration.

d.text: Only running (active or incident) instances qualify; completed and cancelled are explicitly ignored.

explanation: Process instance migration in Operate operates only on running instances. The docs make this concrete: instances that are active or in an incident state can be migrated; everything else is excluded from the migration plan and silently ignored.
